steps.title: Use factored form structure to find the constant
steps.content: In the factored form $$(x-24)(x-30) + c,$$ when $x = 24$ or $x = 30$, one factor becomes zero, leaving $f(x) = c$. The table shows $f(24) = -8$ and $f(30) = -8$, so the constant must be $c = -8$.

steps.title: Identify and verify the correct choice
steps.content:
Only choice B has the form $(x-24)(x-30) - 8$. Verify with the third data point:
$$f(32) = (32-24)(32-30) - 8 = 8 \cdot 2 - 8 = 16 - 8 = 8$$
This matches $f(32) = 8$ from the table. That matches choice B — B is correct.
